title: salix eriocephala
intro: Salix eriocephala, also known as the plains cottonwood or western cottonwood, is a deciduous tree native to the prairies of North America. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Salix eriocephala are believed to help alleviate various health issues, including sore throats, fever, and digestive problems. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made into infusions, tinctures, or salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal benefits.
